bullet_points: The news highlights the trending status of the Meta-Llama-3-8B model on Hugging Face.
It is categorized under key AI/ML domains such as transformers, llama, and text-generation.
The presence of 'safetensors' indicates modern, secure model handling practices.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the trending status of the meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It points to the model's relevance within the transformer and text-generation ecosystem.
The associated tags indicate its connection to major AI frameworks and model formats (transformers, safetensors).

stories.title: A revolutionary cancer treatment could transform autoimmune disease
stories.author: Amber Dance, Knowable Magazine
bullet_points: CAR T cell therapy is being investigated for treating autoimmune diseases.
The therapy aims to 'reset' the immune system, a significant biological mechanism.
This represents a potential paradigm shift in treating complex immune-related conditions.

stories.title: A Unified Generative-AI Framework for Smart Energy Infrastructure: Intelligent Gas Distribution, Utility Billing, Carbon Analytics, and Quantum-Inspired Optimisation
stories.author: Thomas pruefer
bullet_points: Proposes a unified Generative AI framework for modernizing energy infrastructure.
Integrates advanced concepts like smart metering, AI, and quantum-inspired optimization.
Addresses critical utility functions including gas distribution, billing, and carbon analytics.

stories.title: Researcher says Microsoft secretly built a backdoor into BitLocker
stories.author: techspot.com via oceanhaiyang
bullet_points: Allegation claims Microsoft secretly embedded a backdoor within BitLocker encryption.
The story originates from a researcher's claims, suggesting a serious security vulnerability.
This topic is highly relevant to enterprise security and operating system integrity.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of the 'deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1' model on Hugging Face.
It is categorized under text-generation, indicating its primary function as a language model.
The associated tags suggest relevance to transformers, safetensors, and conversational AI.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, has integrated MLX for enhanced performance.
This integration specifically targets Apple Silicon, optimizing AI workloads on Apple hardware.
The feature is currently available in a preview build, indicating ongoing development and optimization.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed to integrate with messaging applications.
It connects users to local AI coding agents.
A key feature is that all processing runs entirely on the user's own device for privacy.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command for easily setting up and running coding tools.
It supports various models, including Claude Code, OpenCode, and Codex, locally or in the cloud.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no environment variables or configuration files.

stories.title: OpenAI gpt-oss-safeguard
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama is integrating OpenAI's gpt-oss-safeguard reasoning models for safety classification tasks.
The models, available in 20B and 120B sizes, are being deployed through a partnership with OpenAI and ROOST.
The models are open-sourced under the permissive Apache 2.0 license, promoting accessible AI safety tools.

stories.title: The US is betting on AI to catch insider trading in prediction markets
stories.author: Kate Knibbs, wired.com
bullet_points: The US Commodity Futures Trading Commission (CFTC) is actively using AI to combat insider trading.
The focus of this AI application is monitoring and analyzing prediction markets for suspicious activity.
This signals a significant regulatory shift towards leveraging advanced AI/ML for financial market integrity.

stories.title: Anthropic’s $1.5B copyright settlement is getting messy as judge delays approval
stories.author: Ashley Belanger
bullet_points: Anthropic is involved in a significant copyright settlement valued at $1.5 billion.
The approval of this historic settlement is facing delays from the judge.
Legal disputes have arisen, with accusations that lawyers rushed the process to secure substantial fees.

stories.title: Designing Datacenter Power Delivery Hierarchies for the AI Era
stories.author: Ricardo Bianchini
bullet_points: AI accelerator demand is driving rapid increases in datacenter rack power density, projected to reach 1MW per deployment by 2027.
High power density presents a critical design challenge, risking stranded power if the delivery hierarchy is not appropriately scaled.
Future datacenter designs must ensure power delivery efficiency and flexibility across extended lifecycles and multiple hardware generations.

stories.title: Prospective multi-pathogen disease forecasting using autonomous LLM-guided tree search
stories.author: Zahra Shamsi
bullet_points: Introduces an autonomous system leveraging LLM-guided tree search for disease forecasting.
Addresses the scalability bottleneck in infectious disease modeling caused by manual expert curation.
Aims to provide prospective, real-time forecasting capabilities for multi-pathogen scenarios.

stories.title: FORGE: Self-Evolving Agent Memory With No Weight Updates via Population Broadcast
stories.author: Marzia Zaman
bullet_points: Introduces FORGE, a method for self-evolving agent memory in LLM agents without requiring weight updates (gradient descent).
Uses a population-based protocol that wraps a Reflexion-style inner loop to convert failed agent trajectories into reusable knowledge artifacts.
The system employs a dedicated reflection agent to generate prompt-injected, natural-language memory for hierarchical ReAct agents.

stories.title: FORGE: Self-Evolving Agent Memory With No Weight Updates via Population Broadcast
stories.author: Marzia Zaman
bullet_points: Introduces FORGE, a novel protocol for self-evolving agent memory in LLM agents.
Achieves knowledge evolution without requiring traditional gradient updates or model weight changes.
Uses a population-based, Reflection-style inner loop to convert failed agent trajectories into reusable knowledge artifacts.

stories.title: A Unified Generative-AI Framework for Smart Energy Infrastructure: Intelligent Gas Distribution, Utility Billing, Carbon Analytics, and Quantum-Inspired Optimisation
stories.author: Thomas pruefer
bullet_points: Proposes a unified Generative AI framework for modernizing energy infrastructure.
Integrates advanced concepts like smart metering, AI, and quantum-inspired optimization.
Addresses critical utility functions including gas distribution, billing, and carbon analytics.

stories.title: Universal Magnetic Structure Prediction from Atomic Coordinates with Near-Experimental Accuracy
stories.author: Mingda Li
bullet_points: Introduces Magnetic Structure Network (MSN), an E(3) equivariant graph neural network.
MSN aims to predict magnetic structures from atomic coordinates with near-experimental accuracy.
Addresses a major challenge in materials science by handling complex noncollinear and incommensurate magnetic orders.

stories.title: FORGE: Self-Evolving Agent Memory With No Weight Updates via Population Broadcast
stories.author: Marzia Zaman
bullet_points: Introduces FORGE, a novel protocol for self-evolving agent memory in LLM agents.
Allows agents to improve decision-making using self-generated, natural-language memory without requiring gradient updates or weight changes.
Employs a population-based, Reflexion-style inner loop where failures are converted into reusable knowledge artifacts.

stories.title: A Unified Generative-AI Framework for Smart Energy Infrastructure: Intelligent Gas Distribution, Utility Billing, Carbon Analytics, and Quantum-Inspired Optimisation
stories.author: Thomas pruefer
bullet_points: Proposes a unified Generative AI framework to modernize energy infrastructure management.
Integrates smart metering, GenAI, and quantum-inspired optimization for utility operations.
Addresses key industry challenges including gas distribution, billing, and carbon analytics.

stories.title: Fully Open Meditron: An Auditable Pipeline for Clinical LLMs
stories.author: Mary-Anne Hartley
bullet_points: Addresses the critical need for auditable and reproducible pipelines in Clinical LLMs (CDSS).
Highlights the current limitation of 'open' models, which often only release weights but hide data provenance and training pipelines.
Introduces 'Fully Open Meditron' as a solution to provide end-to-end transparency in medical AI models.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: bigscience/bloom
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the 'bigscience/bloom' model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under key ML/NLP technologies like transformers and PyTorch.
The pipeline indicates its primary function is text generation.

stories.title: Claude Code with Anthropic API compatibility
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now supports the Anthropic Messages API.
This integration allows users to leverage models like Claude Code within the Ollama ecosystem.
It bridges the gap between open-source local model deployment and proprietary API capabilities.

stories.title: OpenAI Codex with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama enables the use of OpenAI's Codex CLI with open-weight models.
Codex, when integrated via Ollama, can read, modify, and execute code locally.
The integration supports various open-weight alternatives like gpt-oss:20b and gpt-oss:120b.

stories.title: Linus Torvalds says Linux security list is becoming ‘unmanageable’ due to AI bug reports
stories.author: Stevie Bonifield
bullet_points: Linux founder Linus Torvalds reports that the security list is becoming 'unmanageable'.
The primary cause cited is the 'continued flood of AI reports' submitted to the kernel.
Torvalds notes significant duplication in these reports, as different sources find the same issues using similar tools.

stories.title: Revamped Siri will reportedly offer autodeleting chats
stories.author: Terrence O’Brien
bullet_points: Apple is reportedly enhancing Siri to be more chatbot-like in iOS 27.
A key new feature will be the option for users to autodelete Siri chat histories.
The update is framed by Apple as a privacy differentiator against competitors in the AI space.

stories.title: Bug bounty businesses bombarded with AI slop
stories.author: Jamie John, Financial Times
bullet_points: The proliferation of low-quality, AI-generated security vulnerabilities ('AI slop') is impacting bug bounty programs.
The sheer volume of AI-generated submissions is straining the capacity and reward structures of corporate hacking schemes.
This highlights a growing challenge in cybersecurity where automated, low-effort attacks are becoming prevalent.

stories.title: The US space enterprise is desperately waiting for Starship—will it finally deliver?
stories.author: Eric Berger
bullet_points: The US space enterprise's progress is heavily dependent on the success of Starship.
The development of Starship is characterized by extreme volatility, with both significant successes and setbacks.
The article suggests a high level of anticipation and uncertainty regarding Starship's ultimate delivery capability.

stories.title: IVGT: Implicit Visual Geometry Transformer for Neural Scene Representation
stories.author: Jiwen Lu
bullet_points: Introduces IVGT, an Implicit Visual Geometry Transformer designed for 3D scene reconstruction.
Addresses the challenge of reconstructing coherent 3D geometry from unposed, multi-view images.
Overcomes limitations of existing methods (like explicit pointmaps) by implicitly modeling continuous geometry.

stories.title: A Generative AI Framework for Intelligent Utility Billing CO 2 Analytics and Sustainable Resource Optimisation
stories.author: Thomas Pruefer
bullet_points: Proposes a generative AI framework to address the need for detailed, carbon-aware utility billing.
The system integrates a generative AI agent to draft natural-language bills from structured data, adhering to constraints.
It includes a transformer-based forecaster to manage load scheduling against grid stress and emissions constraints.

stories.title: AI-Mediated Communication Can Steer Collective Opinion
stories.author: Sandra Wachter
bullet_points: Investigates how AI-mediated communication influences collective opinion formation online.
Focuses on the gap in research regarding AI's impact on human-to-human opinion dynamics, beyond individual interactions.
Notes the increasing integration of LLMs in platforms like LinkedIn and X for content polishing and context provision.

stories.title: Offline Semantic Guidance for Efficient Vision-Language-Action Policy Distillation
stories.author: Yishun Lu
bullet_points: Addresses the high computational cost of large Vision-Language-Action (VLA) policies used in robotic manipulation.
Introduces VLA-AD, a distillation framework that uses a Vision-Language Model as an offline semantic supervisor.
Aims to transfer knowledge from large, complex 'teacher' VLA models into smaller, efficient 'student' policies for real-time control.

stories.title: Layer Equivalence Is Not a Property of Layers Alone: How You Test Redundancy Changes What You Find
stories.author: Gabriel Garcia
bullet_points: The concept of 'layer equivalence' in transformer models is often misunderstood in the context of model compression.
The study distinguishes between 'replacement' (substitutability) and 'interchange' (commutativity) tests for layer equivalence.
The choice of evaluation protocol (replacement vs. interchange) can lead to significantly different conclusions about which layers are safe to prune in pretrained transformers.

stories.title: Evaluating Design Video Generation: Metrics for Compositional Fidelity
stories.author: Purvanshi Mehta
bullet_points: Addresses the lack of standardized evaluation metrics for generative video models used in design animation.
Highlights the unique constraints of design animation, such as prescribed motion, stable backgrounds, and layout preservation.
Proposes a fully automated evaluation framework structured across four key dimensions to assess compositional fidelity.

stories.title: A Generative AI Framework for Intelligent Utility Billing CO 2 Analytics and Sustainable Resource Optimisation
stories.author: Thomas Pruefer
bullet_points: Proposes a generative AI framework to address the need for detailed, carbon-aware utility billing.
The system integrates a generative AI agent to draft natural-language billing statements from structured data.
It includes a transformer-based forecaster to manage load scheduling against grid stress and emissions constraints.

stories.title: AI-Mediated Communication Can Steer Collective Opinion
stories.author: Sandra Wachter
bullet_points: The research investigates how AI-mediated communication influences collective opinion formation online.
It addresses a gap in prior work by focusing on human-to-human communication mediated by LLMs, rather than just human-AI interaction.
The study examines the role of generative AI (like LLMs used on LinkedIn and X) in shaping group discourse.

stories.title: Dynamics-Level Watermarking of Flow Matching Models with Random Codes
stories.author: Shuchan Wang
bullet_points: Introduces a novel dynamics-level watermarking technique for generative models, specifically flow matching models.
The watermark is embedded directly into the learned continuous dynamics (velocity field) rather than model weights or outputs.
The method utilizes random coding over a continuous channel, allowing for black-box detection queries.

stories.title: Layer Equivalence Is Not a Property of Layers Alone: How You Test Redundancy Changes What You Find
stories.author: Gabriel Garcia
bullet_points: The paper highlights a critical distinction between two methods for testing layer redundancy in transformers: 'replacement' and 'interchange'.
These two tests, while related, do not always yield the same results regarding layer equivalence for model compression.
The discrepancy (protocol gap) can significantly alter which transformer layers are deemed safe to prune, impacting compression strategies.

stories.title: Artificial Aphasias in Lesioned Language Models
stories.author: Cory Shain
bullet_points: The research proposes an 'aphasia-inspired' technique to study the functional organization of Large Language Models (LMs).
The method involves 'lesioning' (zeroing out) specific model parameters and observing the resulting changes.
The impact of these lesions is measured against established clinical aphasia symptom profiles to draw parallels between brain function and LM behavior.

stories.title: A Generative AI Framework for Intelligent Utility Billing CO 2 Analytics and Sustainable Resource Optimisation
stories.author: Thomas Pruefer
bullet_points: Proposes a generative AI framework to address the need for carbon-aware utility billing.
The system uses a generative AI agent to draft natural-language billing statements from structured data.
It integrates a transformer-based forecaster to manage load scheduling against grid stress and emissions.

stories.title: AI-Mediated Communication Can Steer Collective Opinion
stories.author: Sandra Wachter
bullet_points: The research investigates how AI mediation influences collective opinion formation in online discussions.
It addresses a gap in prior work, which primarily focused on individual opinion shaping during human-AI interactions.
The study examines the role of LLMs in platforms like LinkedIn and X where AI polishes and contextualizes user content.

stories.title: Layer Equivalence Is Not a Property of Layers Alone: How You Test Redundancy Changes What You Find
stories.author: Gabriel Garcia
bullet_points: The paper addresses a critical ambiguity in evaluating transformer layer redundancy for model compression.
It distinguishes between 'replacement' (substitutability) and 'interchange' (commutativity) tests for layer equivalence.
The study highlights that the choice of evaluation protocol (replacement vs. interchange) can drastically alter which layers are deemed safe to prune in pretrained transformers.

stories.title: Artificial Aphasias in Lesioned Language Models
stories.author: Cory Shain
bullet_points: The research proposes an 'aphasia-inspired' technique to study the functional organization of Language Models (LMs).
This method involves 'lesioning' (zeroing out) specific model parameters to mimic brain damage.
The impact of these parameter interventions is measured against clinical aphasia symptom profiles to understand LM structure.

stories.title: Argus: Evidence Assembly for Scalable Deep Research Agents
stories.author: Xinyu Wang
bullet_points: Introduces 'Argus,' a method designed to improve evidence assembly in deep research agents.
Addresses the limitation of current parallel search methods, which often duplicate evidence instead of completing a comprehensive answer.
Aims to overcome diminishing returns and context window limitations in state-of-the-art deep research systems.

stories.title: Confirming Correct, Missing the Rest: LLM Tutoring Agents Struggle Where Feedback Matters Most
stories.author: Tiffany Barnes
bullet_points: The study evaluates LLM tutoring agents on their ability to provide nuanced feedback, specifically distinguishing between correct, valid-but-suboptimal, and incorrect solutions.
It addresses a critical gap in Intelligent Tutoring Systems (ITS) by testing LLMs' diagnostic precision, which is vital for their integration into educational technology.
The research utilizes a large-scale benchmark (10,836 pairs) in propositional logic, grounded by knowledge-graph-derived truth.

stories.title: I Ran AI Models Directly in the Browser and Measured What It Did to Core Web Vitals
stories.author: mspk97
bullet_points: The article investigates running AI models directly within the web browser.
It measures the performance impact of in-browser AI on Core Web Vitals.
The context is the growing trend of shipping AI features (e.g., sentiment analysis) client-side.

stories.title: 🚀 Democratizing Frontier AI for Bharat: Gemma 4’s Edge Capabilities in Low-Resource Environments
stories.author: pravesh_sudha_3c2b0c2b5e0
bullet_points: The article discusses the application of Google's open-weight Gemma 4 model.
The focus is on enabling 'Frontier AI' capabilities specifically for 'Bharat' (India).
A key theme is the model's performance and utility in 'Low-Resource Environments'.

stories.title: OpenSRE: Build Your Own AI Incident-Investigation Agent
stories.author: arshtechpro
bullet_points: The article introduces 'OpenSRE,' an AI agent designed for incident investigation.
It addresses a gap in current AI tools, which are often limited to code generation in editors.
The focus is on automating complex, high-stress tasks like post-incident analysis.
